text:
Modern society has long recognized that children occupy a unique position within the legal and judicial systems. Because law serves as a reflection of evolving societal values, there is an increasing awareness that legal frameworks must be specifically tailored to children at both normative and institutional levels. This specialized protection is particularly vital within the repressive branches of the law, namely criminal and misdemeanor justice. 

Adopting the contemporary terminology of juvenile justice, this paper employs the phrase "child in conflict with the law" to examine the legal status of minors. It provides a comprehensive overview of the normative foundations and future perspectives regarding children in the justice system, analyzing international standards and their influence alongside the specific needs and current state of Croatian society. Furthermore, the discussion extends beyond the child as a perpetrator to address the critical legal position of the child as a victim of a criminal offense.
